# Add Controls

## Lesson Goal

Use sliders to change frequency, duration, and volume.

## What to Notice

Controls belong in one cell, and their `.value` is read later. This is the basic Marimo reactive pattern.

## Work With the Notebook

- [Open the notebook source](https://github.com/buildLittleWorlds/marimo-audio-self-tutorial/blob/main/notebooks/02_wave_controls/notebook.py)
- [Open the editable browser notebook](https://buildlittleworlds.github.io/marimo-audio-self-tutorial/notebooks/02_wave_controls/)

Run locally:

```bash
uv run marimo edit notebooks/02_wave_controls/notebook.py
```

## Feedback Lens

Check whether the student understands why changing a slider re-runs downstream cells without manually re-running the notebook.

## Instructor Notes

Use this lesson as a small checkpoint. Before asking students to add features,
ask them to name the input, the transformation, the output, and the current
limitation. That habit will matter more in later transcription notebooks than
any one line of code.
